97.reason: [The function name 'delete' is a reserved keyword in C++ and may cause compilation issues with some compilers even in C mode, especially if headers include C++-style macros or if compiled in mixed environments. It's unsafe to use 'delete' as an identifier in C when compatibility or certain compiler flags are involved.]

126.reason: [Calling a function named 'delete' which is problematic due to being a reserved word in C++. Even in pure C, some compilers or build environments may flag this as an error or warning, leading to compilation failure.]
